pr.title: Add Windows-first Tauri launcher for addon install/update, rollback, and release-driven delivery
pr.body:
This PR introduces a dedicated launcher to replace the Electron-based direction with a Tauri architecture, while keeping the existing plugin build/release flow intact. The launcher now handles install/update lifecycle concerns (preflight, backup, rollback, release fetch, checksum verification, and launch) from a single Windows-first app boundary.

- **Launcher architecture (separate ownership boundary)**
  - Added a standalone `launcher/` Tauri app so plugin code and existing DLL packaging remain decoupled.
  - Implemented privileged operations in Rust commands (filesystem/process/network) with a minimal frontend for operator actions.

- **Installer/update lifecycle**
  - Added local-zip install flow and “latest release” install flow against GitHub Releases (`bbmod.zip`).
  - Added preflight and dry-run paths before mutation.
  - Added post-install verification and tracked backup/rollback for addon payload files.

- **Security/reliability controls**
  - Enforced archive extraction allowlist (`addons/**`, `dinput8.dll`, `dinput8.pdb`, `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`).
  - Added path validation (absolute paths), checksum verification (explicit SHA-256 or release asset-derived), and structured JSONL logging.
  - Centralized executable candidate handling and status warnings from shared constants.

- **UX surface**
  - Replaced template UI with focused panels for:
    - status + warnings
    - install path settings
    - local zip preflight/install
    - latest release install
    - rollback
    - launch
    - log viewing
  - Added explicit destructive-action confirmations and VC++ runtime reminder.

- **Release/docs integration**
  - Updated root docs to make launcher-first installation the default with manual zip fallback.
  - Added launcher maintainer/developer docs.
  - Extended AppVeyor artifact publishing to include launcher bundle zip when present, without changing existing `bbmod.zip` packaging behavior.

```rust
#[tauri::command]
fn install_latest_release(app: AppHandle, request: InstallLatestRequest) -> Result<InstallResult, String> {
    let release = fetch_latest_release_internal()?;
    let zip_destination = downloads_dir(&app)?.join(format!("bbmod-{}.zip", release.tag_name));
    download_to_path(&release.bbmod_zip_url, &zip_destination)?;
    // optional SHA-256 verification...
    install_from_zip_internal(&app, &zip_destination, &target, Some(release.tag_name), dry_run)
}
```
pr.author: Copilot
